LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO THE PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. IT IS PREMATURE TO DISMISS THE CLAIM AT THIS POINT OF THE LITIGATION. AS TO THE VIOLATION OF CALIFORNIA LABOR CODE SEC. 970 CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE MORE FACTS AS TO THE MOVE INTO TEMPORARY HOUSING INCLUDING THE CHANGE IN LOCALE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047 (302/MJM)
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-15-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER RE: PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. TO STATE A CLAIM FOR A VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE SECTION 970, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THAT A PERSON HAS KNOWINGLY MADE FALSE REPRESENTATIONS THAT "DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY'INFLUENCE, PERSUADE, OR ENGAGE ANY PERSON TO CHANGE FROM ONE PLACE TO ANOTHER IN THIS STATE'OR FROM ANY PLACE WITHIN THE STATE TO ANY PLACE OUTSIDE, FOR THE PURPOSE OF WORKING IN ANY BRANCH OF LABOR . '" HERE, PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANT (HIS EMPLOYER) MADE FALSE REPRESENTATIONS OF EMPLOYMENT IN GEORGIA, AND THAT, IN RELIANCE ON DEFENDANT'S MISREPRESENTATIONS, PLAINTIFF SOLD HIS HOUSE IN CALIFORNIA, MOVED TO TEMPORARY HOUSING, FOREWENT ENROLLING HIS CHILDREN IN SCHOOL IN CALIFORNIA, ENROLLED HIS CHILDREN IN SCHOOL IN GEORGIA, ENTERED INTO A CONTRACT FOR THE PURCHASE OF A HOME IN GEORGIA, AND MOVED TO GEORGIA. THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT PHYSICALLY COMPLETE THE MOVE TO GEORGIA UNTIL AFTER HE WAS INFORMED THAT HE WAS TERMINATED FROM EMPLOYMENT IS NOT DISPOSITIVE OF WHETHER DEFENDANT'S MISREPRESENTATIONS "DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY'INFLUENCE[D], PERSUADE[D] OR ENGAGE[D]" PLAINTIFF TO RELOCATE AS REQUIRED BY THE STATUTE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

Defendant Yellowpages.Com Llc'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Yellowpages.Com LLC's Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication taken off calendar; duplicate entry for purposes of tentative ruling. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jeremy Pasternak, of Law Offices of Jeremy Pasternak, Tel. 415.693.0300, for plaintiffs;
Janavi Thanawala, of Law Offices of Jeremy Pasternak, Tel. 415.693.0300, for plaintiffs;
Jesse M. Jauregui, of Alston & Bird LLP, Tel. 213.576.1000, for defendants;
Nicole C. Rivas, of Alston & Bird LLP, Tel. 213.576.1000, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. The court cannot rule as a matter of law that the unclean hands or after-acquired evidence doctrines completely bar plaintiff's claims. First, the defenses raise factual issues that the trier of fact should resolve. Plaintiff correctly cites Ford v. Sup. Crt. (1959) 176 Cal.App.2d 754, 759, for the proposition that a jury will resolve the unclean hands defense because plaintiff brings an action at law. Plaintiff's declaration (pars. 18-24) explains that his role in the resurrection of Pallina Partners was limited and could even benefit defendant. Second, in Camp v. Jeffer, Mangels, Butler & Marmaro (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 620, 638, the court explained that the after-acquired evidence doctrine could bar a claim in its entirety-rather than merely limit damages-where the employee submitted a false employment application and government-imposed requirements disqualified the plaintiff from employment. This action does not involve government-imposed requirements. The court denies summary adjudication on the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant causes of action. See Sheppard v. Morgan Keegan & Co. (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 61, 67. Even if Guz v. Bechtel Inc. (2004) 24 Cal.4th 317, casts doubt on Sheppard, an at-will employment agreement does not give an employer the right to mislead an employee into moving across the country. The court denies summary adjudication on the fraud/concealment/nondisclosure claims. Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production. Defendant's separate statement of undisputed facts focuses on whether defendant misrepresented Mr. Simon's role as plaintiff's replacement. Defendant does not sufficiently address plaintiff's theory of liability based upon the relocation promises. Plaintiff's evidence establishes that defendant knew that plaintiff would not be relocated, but made misleading statements. (Plaintiff's additional facts, 41, 42, 44, Exs D & E to Thanawala dec.). A constructive fraud/concealment cause of action can be established without a special relationship where there is exclusive knowledge, partial suppression, or active concealment. See Witkin, Torts, CA Law, Vol. 5, sec. 798-799 (2005). Mr. Collins made misleading statements while knowing that the relocation was not verified. The court denies summary adjudication on the Labor Code sec. 970 cause of action. At paragraph 5 of his declaration, Plaintiff declares that he sought to move based on defendant's representations, not his son's lacrosse future. Plaintiff's movement to rental property prior to the termination could support the cause of action. The court denies summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages. Defendant's separate statement focuses on Mr. Collins and states that he was not an officer, director, or managing agent. The defendant does not sufficiently show that Ms. Gentry was not a managing agent. The Court will rule on evidentiary objections and issue an order. Judge Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/SB)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JEREMEY PASTERNAK, TEL. NO. 693-0300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NICOLE C. RIVAS OF ALSTON & BIRD LLP, TEL. NO. (213)576-1021 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. To state a claim for a violation of Labor Code section 970, Plaintiff must allege that a person has knowingly made false representations that "directly or indirectly'influence, persuade, or engage any person to change from one place to another in this State'or from any place within the State to any place outside, for the purpose of working in any branch of labor . '" Here, Plaintiff has alleged that Defendant (his employer) made false representations of employment in Georgia, and that, in reliance on Defendant's misrepresentations, Plaintiff sold his house in California, moved to temporary housing, forewent enrolling his children in school in California, enrolled his children in school in Georgia, entered into a contract for the purchase of a home in Georgia, and moved to Georgia. The fact that Plaintiff did not physically complete the move to Georgia until after he was informed that he was terminated from employment is not dispositive of whether Defendant's misrepresentations "directly or indirectly'influence[d], persuade[d] or engage[d]" Plaintiff to relocate as required by the statute. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicole C. Rivas, Esq. of Alston & Bird LLP, Tel. 213.576.1021, for defendant and moving party;
Tessa Peralta, Esq. of the Law Offices of Jeremy Pasternak, Tel. 415.693.0300, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT YELLOWPAGES.COM LLC's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled as to the promissory estoppel as an alternative to the breach of contract cause of action. It is premature to dismiss the claim at this point of the litigation. As to the violation of California Labor Code sec. 970 cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend to allege more facts as to the move into temporary housing including the change in locale. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
